audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Abraham's later family, inheritance separation, Abraham's death/burial, Ishmael genealogy and death, Isaac/Rebekah barrenness prayer, twins oracle, Jacob/Esau birth wordplay, and birthright sale. Final source-critical review may refine genealogy/geography.

verses.restored: Abraham took another woman, and her name was Keturah.

familiar.text: Then again Abraham took a wife, and her name was Keturah.

verses.restored: She bore him Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.

familiar.text: And she bare him Zimran, and Jokshan, and Medan...

verses.restored: Jokshan fathered Sheba and Dedan. The sons of Dedan were Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.

familiar.text: And Jokshan begat Sheba, and Dedan...

verses.restored: The sons of Midian were Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All these were the sons of Keturah.

familiar.text: And the sons of Midian; Ephah, and Epher...

verses.restored: Abraham gave all that he had to Isaac.

familiar.text: And Abraham gave all that he had unto Isaac.

notes.title: Isaac as heir
notes.body: The inheritance line is concentrated in Isaac.

verses.restored: To the sons of Abraham’s concubines, Abraham gave gifts, and while he was still living he sent them away from Isaac his son, eastward, to the land of the east.

familiar.text: But unto the sons of the concubines, which Abraham had, Abraham gave gifts...

notes.title: Sent eastward
notes.body: Other sons receive gifts but are sent away from Isaac, preserving the covenant inheritance line.

verses.restored: These are the days of the years of Abraham’s life that he lived: one hundred seventy-five years.

familiar.text: And these are the days of the years of Abraham's life which he lived...

verses.restored: Abraham expired and died in a good old age, old and full, and was gathered to his people.

familiar.text: Then Abraham gave up the ghost, and died in a good old age...

notes.title: Gathered to his people
notes.body: A death idiom suggesting joining ancestors or kin beyond burial alone.

verses.restored: Isaac and Ishmael his sons buried him in the cave of Machpelah, in the field of Ephron son of Zohar the Hittite, before Mamre,

familiar.text: And his sons Isaac and Ishmael buried him in the cave of Machpelah...

notes.title: Isaac and Ishmael together
notes.body: Both sons bury Abraham, briefly reuniting the separated lines.

verses.restored: the field that Abraham bought from the sons of Heth. There Abraham was buried, and Sarah his woman.

familiar.text: The field which Abraham purchased of the sons of Heth: there was Abraham buried, and Sarah his wife.

verses.restored: After Abraham’s death, the Divine blessed Isaac his son. Isaac dwelled by Beer-lahai-roi.

familiar.text: And it came to pass after the death of Abraham, that God blessed his son Isaac...

verses.restored: These are the generations of Ishmael, Abraham’s son, whom Hagar the Egyptian, Sarah’s slave-woman, bore to Abraham.

familiar.text: Now these are the generations of Ishmael, Abraham's son...

verses.restored: These are the names of the sons of Ishmael, by their names, according to their generations: the firstborn of Ishmael, Nebaioth; then Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,

familiar.text: And these are the names of the sons of Ishmael, by their names...

verses.restored: Mishma, Dumah, Massa,

familiar.text: And Mishma, and Dumah, and Massa,

verses.restored: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.

familiar.text: Hadar, and Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah:

verses.restored: These are the sons of Ishmael, and these are their names by their villages and by their encampments, twelve princes according to their peoples.

familiar.text: These are the sons of Ishmael... twelve princes according to their nations.

notes.title: Twelve princes
notes.body: This fulfills the blessing spoken over Ishmael in Genesis 17.

verses.restored: These are the years of the life of Ishmael: one hundred thirty-seven years. He expired and died and was gathered to his people.

familiar.text: And these are the years of the life of Ishmael, an hundred and thirty and seven years...

verses.restored: They dwelled from Havilah to Shur, which is before Egypt as you go toward Asshur. He settled before the face of all his brothers.

familiar.text: And they dwelt from Havilah unto Shur, that is before Egypt...

notes.title: Before the face of his brothers
notes.body: The phrase recalls the prophecy over Ishmael in Genesis 16.

verses.restored: These are the generations of Isaac, Abraham’s son: Abraham fathered Isaac.

familiar.text: And these are the generations of Isaac, Abraham's son: Abraham begat Isaac:

notes.title: Isaac toledot begins
notes.body: The narrative now turns from Abraham’s death and Ishmael’s line to Isaac’s line.

verses.restored: Isaac was forty years old when he took Rebekah, daughter of Bethuel the Aramean from Paddan-Aram, sister of Laban the Aramean, to be his woman.

familiar.text: And Isaac was forty years old when he took Rebekah to wife...

verses.restored: Isaac pleaded with the Eternal opposite his woman, because she was barren. The Eternal was moved by his plea, and Rebekah his woman conceived.

familiar.text: And Isaac intreated the LORD for his wife, because she was barren...

notes.title: Opposite his woman
notes.body: Lenokhach can mean opposite, in front of, or on behalf of. The scene centers prayer for Rebekah’s barrenness.

verses.restored: The children struggled together within her, and she said, If it is so, why am I this? And she went to inquire of the Eternal.

familiar.text: And the children struggled together within her; and she said, If it be so, why am I thus?...

notes.title: Inquire of the Eternal
notes.body: Rebekah seeks divine interpretation of her pregnancy directly.

verses.restored: The Eternal said to her, Two nations are in your womb, and two peoples from your insides shall be separated. One people shall be stronger than the other, and the older shall serve the younger.

familiar.text: And the LORD said unto her, Two nations are in thy womb...

notes.title: Older shall serve younger
notes.body: The oracle reverses normal primogeniture expectations.

verses.restored: When her days to give birth were completed, look, twins were in her womb.

familiar.text: And when her days to be delivered were fulfilled, behold, there were twins...

verses.restored: The first came out red, all of him like a hairy cloak, and they called his name Esau.

familiar.text: And the first came out red, all over like an hairy garment...

notes.title: Red and hairy
notes.body: The description prepares wordplay with Edom/red and Esau’s hairy identity.

verses.restored: Afterward his brother came out, and his hand was grasping Esau’s heel, so his name was called Jacob. Isaac was sixty years old when she bore them.

familiar.text: And after that came his brother out, and his hand took hold on Esau's heel...

notes.title: Jacob and heel
notes.body: Jacob, Yaakov, is connected with aqev, heel. The name will later carry overtones of grasping/supplanting.

verses.restored: The boys grew. Esau became a man knowing hunting, a man of the field; Jacob was a complete/quiet man, dwelling in tents.

familiar.text: And the boys grew: and Esau was a cunning hunter, a man of the field...

notes.title: Complete/quiet man
notes.body: Tam can mean complete, sound, simple, or quiet. The contrast is field/hunting versus tents/dwelling.

verses.restored: Isaac loved Esau, because game was in his mouth; but Rebekah loved Jacob.

familiar.text: And Isaac loved Esau, because he did eat of his venison: but Rebekah loved Jacob.

notes.title: In his mouth
notes.body: The phrase likely means because Isaac ate Esau’s game, but the Hebrew idiom is vivid.

verses.restored: Jacob boiled stew, and Esau came in from the field, and he was exhausted.

familiar.text: And Jacob sod pottage: and Esau came from the field, and he was faint:

verses.restored: Esau said to Jacob, Please let me gulp down some of that red-red, for I am exhausted. Therefore his name was called Edom.

familiar.text: And Esau said to Jacob, Feed me, I pray thee, with that same red pottage...

notes.title: Red-red / Edom
notes.body: The wordplay links red stew, Esau’s redness, and Edom.

verses.restored: Jacob said, Sell me your birthright today.

familiar.text: And Jacob said, Sell me this day thy birthright.

verses.restored: Esau said, Look, I am going to die; what is this birthright to me?

familiar.text: And Esau said, Behold, I am at the point to die: and what profit shall this birthright do to me?

verses.restored: Jacob said, Swear to me today. He swore to him, and sold his birthright to Jacob.

familiar.text: And Jacob said, Swear to me this day; and he sware unto him...

verses.restored: Jacob gave Esau bread and lentil stew. He ate, drank, rose, and went. So Esau despised the birthright.

familiar.text: Then Jacob gave Esau bread and pottage of lentiles; and he did eat and drink...

notes.title: Despised the birthright
notes.body: Bazah means despise, treat as worthless, or hold in contempt. The narrative judges Esau’s action sharply.
